title: centaurea tabriziana
intro: Centaurea tabriziana, a perennial herb of the daisy family, is native to the mountainous regions of the Middle East and is also known as Tabriz Cornflower. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and saponins. Studies have shown that Centaurea tabriziana may have various health benefits, including antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, as well as potential anti-cancer properties. The plant can be prepared as an infusion or tincture, and it is also used in herbal teas and as a topical treatment for wounds and skin irritations.
